2606.25450.title: The Generalization Spectrum: A Chromatographic Approach to Evaluating Learning Algorithms
2606.25450.paper_summary: Traditional evaluations measure a learning algorithm's final performance on an i.i.d. test set, reducing learning to a single aggregate score. This approach obscures a fundamental question: to what extent does learning from a specific example generalize to others? Such per-sample generalization, akin to learning by analogy in human cognition, captures how far the knowledge extracted from one example can transfer, yet remains invisible to standard benchmarks. We introduce the Generalization Spectrum, an evaluation framework designed to expose this hidden dimension. For each training example, we construct a controlled suite of test variants arranged by increasing transfer distance, from exact recall to implementation transfer across languages, context transfer under complete narrative re-framing, category-matched in-domain problems, and an unpaired baseline. By tracking performance across these distances, we reveal not just whether an algorithm learns, but how far that learning extends. We instantiate this framework on competitive programming, using a selection-and-synthesis pipeline seeded with recent problems to mitigate contamination. We first compare three canonical learning paradigms under matched memorization. RL converts memorization into near-transfer more efficiently than SFT-family baselines, while ICL exhibits strong but correspondence-dependent transfer. We then use the Spectrum to diagnose within-family variants. The resulting profiles show that local gains need not expand the generalization radius: abstractions and hints mainly lift local transfer, RFT preserves a stronger far-transfer tail than reference SFT, and self-distillation or hint-assisted RL can reduce far transfer even when local transfer or optimization improves.
2606.25450.paper_summary_zh: 傳統評估測量學習算法在獨立同分佈測試集上的最終表現，將學習簡化為單一的綜合評分。這種方法掩蓋了一個基本問題：從特定示例學習在多大程度上可以推廣到其他示例？這種逐樣本的推廣，類似於人類認知中的類比學習，捕捉了從一個示例中提取的知識能夠轉移的範圍，但對於標準基準來說仍然是不可見的。我們引入了泛化光譜，一個旨在揭示這一隱藏維度的評估框架。對於每個訓練示例，我們構建了一套受控的測試變體，按轉移距離遞增排列，從精確回憶到跨語言的實施轉移、在完全敘事重構下的上下文轉移、類別匹配的內部問題，以及一個未配對的基線。通過跟踪這些距離上的表現，我們揭示了算法不僅學會了什麼，還學會了多遠。我們在競賽編程上實例化這一框架，使用一個由近期問題播種的選擇和綜合管道來減少污染。我們首先在匹配記憶的情況下比較三種經典學習範式。強化學習比SFT系列基線更有效地將記憶轉換為近距離轉移，而即時學習則展現出強勁但依賴於對應的轉移。我們然後使用光譜來診斷同家族變體。結果顯示，局部增益不必擴大泛化半徑：抽象和提示主要提升局部轉移，RFT保留了比參考SFT更強的遠距轉移尾，而自我蒸餾或提示輔助的強化學習即使在局部轉移或優化改善的情況下也可能減少遠距轉移。
2606.25450.author: Jinghan Zhang et.al.
2606.25450.authors: Jinghan Zhang, Zerui Cheng, Shiqi Chen, Ge Zhang, Wenhao Huang, Jiashuo Liu, Junxian He, Tianle Cai
